name: High amount above benchmark
description: Claims above the expected procedure benchmark should be reviewed for documentation and pricing support.

name: Rapid repeat billing
description: Repeated claims within seven days may indicate duplicate billing, unbundling, or unnecessary utilization.

name: High prior claim frequency
description: Unusually high prior claim volume can indicate waste, abuse, or operational billing errors.

name: High-risk provider history
description: Provider-level outlier history should increase audit priority but must not be treated as final fraud proof.

name: Diagnosis-procedure mismatch
description: Procedure and diagnosis combinations should be clinically plausible and supported by documentation.

name: DME necessity
description: Durable medical equipment claims require diagnosis support, physician order, and documentation of medical necessity.
